entry.title: Montly credit statistic when using Joule for Consultant
entry.content:
Hello everyone,

I'm try to using SAP Joule for Consultant but I have a question about how I know Montly Credit Statistics Using License about SAP_JOULE_PREMIUM_CONSULTANT? And how I know who uses the most and who use the least credit?

So anyone help me to answer this? Please help me to answer. Thank you so much!

entry.title: How do I make Joule context aware
entry.content:
Hi everyone,

I’m working with SAP Build (React + UI5) and Joule Studio, and I’m trying to make Joule responses aware of user interactions in the UI.

For example, if a user clicks on a specific node/component, I want Joule to understand that context when the user interacts with it.

Is there a recommended way to pass or associate UI-driven context with a Joule Skill? Can Joule be invoked with predefined context or parameters from the UI?

Any guidance or best practices would be appreciated.

Thanks!

entry.title: Joule Studio Integration with Mapbox MCP
entry.content:
Hi Team,

We are working on a scenario of integration of Joule studio with Mapbox MCP. We are trying to directly use the hosted environment created for the MCP as per the documentation:

MCP Server | API Docs | Mapbox

We have created destination in BTP for the same as below:

Added the required token as authorization as below:

Additional properties as below:

However, while trying to consume in a Joule Studio, it gives up a error as below:

Can you please advise if there is any issue with regards to configuration from my end which is missing or where the issue lies. Any helpful document with regards to this is appreciated.

Thanks

Tirthankar

entry.title: Joule Studio Document grounding-Resource showing pending
entry.content:
Hi All,

We are working on the document grounding part of the Joule studio. We have a AI core instance in a different subaccount and we have created a destination pointing to ai core based on the service key generated with additional property configured.

We have created a instance of Object Store and generated key with both read and write access. We are able to access Object store in Amazon S3 bucket using winSCP and put or access the list.

We have created a destination with all the required details for object store along with additional details. Details of destination are as follows:

{"exportTime":"2026-03-25 02:52:41.695405942",
"destination":
{
"Name":"OBJECT_STORE_TC",
"Type":"HTTP",
"Description":"",
"URL":"https://s3-eu-central-1.amazonaws.com",
"ProxyType":"Internet",
"Authentication":"OAuth2ClientCredentials",
"region":"eu-central-1",
"clientId":"AKIAYM5H3IAUXU6RRQXA",
"clientSecret":"<removed>",
"tokenServiceURL":"https://s3-eu-central-1.amazonaws.com",
"tokenServiceURLType":"Dedicated",
"bucket":"Obtained from Object store service key",
"username":"hcp-s3-4b72c380-b2cd-4899-8115-86bfeb195418",
"sap-joule-studio-document-grounding-object-store":"true",
"type":"S3"
}}
However, on trying to establish connectivity using Joule studio document grounding , it remains in status pending. If i go to ai core and check for the resource group, there also i can see it is not created.
Screen shot in Joule Studio:

Screen shot in AI Launchpad where it shows in processing:

Can anyone please help me on whether there is anything wrong that i am doing in.

@Dan_Wroblewski

Thanks

Tirthankar

entry.title: Difficulty setting up Joule in BTP trial account
entry.content:
Hi there. I'm new to SAP and trying to work out whether it can fit the use case I have in mind.

I'd like to use SAP Joule with some generic business trigger (e.g. low stock) to run a Joule Skill, one step of which will be running an action, which calls an API. I want to prove this is possible in a trial account for now.

I have set up a trial BTP account, and got as far as activating SAP Build Process Automation, and SAP Build Apps in case it is relevant, however when in the lobby, if I click create I only see 'Application', 'Automated Process', and 'Business Site'. When I created a trial directly for Build Apps I was able to see a 'Joule Agent and Skill' option (However with this account I was not able to set up destinations correctly, hence moving to the BTP trial account).

How can I enable Joule on this account so that I can create this flow where some generic event trigger a skill, calling and action, calling an API?

Thanks

entry.title: AI Unit Cost per Request for Sales Order Creation (S/4HANA Public Cloud)
entry.content:
Hi SAP Community,

I would like to confirm my understanding of AI Unit–based pricing for the following feature:

SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition – Sales order creation from unstructured data

From the Discovery Center, I see:

  • AI Units per request: 0.1
  • Price per AI Unit: EUR 7

Based on this, my understanding is:

  • Cost per request = 0.1 × 7 = EUR 0.7
  • Therefore, if I create 100 sales orders, the total cost would be:
    → 100 × 0.7 = EUR 70

Could you please confirm if this calculation is correct?

Also, I would like to clarify:

  • Does one “request” always correspond to one sales order creation?
  • Are there any additional AI Unit consumptions (e.g., document parsing, retries, or workflow steps) that should be considered?

Additionally, I checked the AI Unit price list for my TDD environment, but I could not find this specific feature listed there.
Could you please advise where I can confirm the exact AI Unit pricing for this feature in TDD?

Thanks in advance for your support!

entry.title: Activating Document Grounding with Joule in SAP BTP
entry.content:
Dear community,

I have a question about activating Document Grounding in SAP BTP: Do AI Core units, Joule, and Document Grounding need to be in the same global account or the same subaccount?

In the prerequisites, the documentation says: “You’ve added the entitlements for Document Grounding to the subaccount where you’ve subscribed to Joule,” and at the same time: “You’ve purchased the AI Unit SKU (8019164) and AI units. After you’ve done so, the entitlements for Document Grounding are automatically added to your global account.”

Does this mean that all three - AI units, Joule, and the Document Grounding instance - must be in the same global account and subaccount? Or are there any exceptions?

entry.title: Joule Destination not appearing in IAS
entry.content:
We are trying to setup the Joule for the S/4HANA Private cloud systems.

Followed the SAP guide but when we are trying to Configure Identity Provisioning Service it does not gives name of the destination in the dropdown in the IAS. Attached is the screenshot for the same.

Can you please help with it

entry.title: How to fix Backend error in ISLM scenario: SD_INT_AI_GPT in Joule?
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

When I ask Joule questions related to sales, it returns the error:
‘Backend error in ISLM scenario: SD_INT_AI_GPT. Check the intelligent scenario maintenance in transaction ISLM_CONN_MAP.’

When I then go into S/4HANA and run transaction ISLM_CONN_MAP, I see another error — ‘HTTP failure, processing failed, invalid state, invalid timeout, or others’ — when I click Check Connection.

 

Has anyone encountered a similar issue before, or have any suggestions on how to troubleshoot this?

(Some of my questions are: Has the goods of sales order 12345678 arrived? When was the sales order 12345678 created?)

(I am integrating Joule with S/4HANA PCE.)

Any tips or guidance would be highly appreciated. Thank you in advance!

Joule SAP S/4HANA

entry.title: Joule Exceeds Message Size 10KB
entry.content:
Help!

After integrating Joule with S/4HANA Private Cloud. I'm experiencing inconsistency with Joule’s responses on Fiori.

For example, when I ask Joule, “Find me suppliers containing XXX,” it works as expected. However, when I ask the same question for a different supplier, such as “Find me suppliers containing YYY,” Joule stops responding. It thinks for a bit and then returns nothing.

I then created a Joule Skill in Joule Studio to recreate the same scenario and ran into the same issue, but with better logging.

Based on the logs, the response says that the message size exceeded the 10KB limit. 

So, this limit is everywhere? If so, how can we increase the limit?

I know with building agents you can use $top or pagination or anything to stay under. But what about the actual Joule with S/4HANA Private?

Thanks in advance.

entry.title: Integrating Custom Joule Skill from SAP BTP Sub acc A into Joule for S/4HANA Public Cloud (Sub acc B
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

I am currently working with SAP BTP Joule and facing a challenge in integrating a custom Joule skill across subaccounts.

🔹Current Setup

  • Subaccount A
    • Created a custom Joule skill/agent using Joule Studio
    • Deployed and integrated it with SAP Build Work Zone
    • The skill is working successfully within Work Zone
  • Subaccount B
    • Enabled Joule for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition
    • Joule is active and usable within S/4HANA Public Cloud

🔹Requirement

I want to integrate the custom Joule skill developed in Subaccount A to  Joule in S/4HANA Public Cloud (Subaccount B) so that it is also available inside Joule in S/4HANA Public Cloud (Subaccount B).

How can I achieve this? Any documentation or recommended architecture for this scenario?

entry.title: Can't open Joule in Public Cloud: login.microsoftonline.com refused to connect
entry.content:
After successfull integration of Joule and working with it for a week, one technical issue has appeared. It doesn't work only for one user in the system.

"login.microsoftonline.com" refused to connect

I've tried:
- to use google Chrome

-to use Microsoft Edge

-to delete cookies and cash

-to use inkognito window

and nothing help

Dear community, 

i will be very grateful for your help 🙂
@Raja_Gupta1

entry.title: Joule Studio Skill Fails with 403 (Access Denied) During Execution
entry.content:
Hello SAP Community,

We are currently working on a Joule Studio skill in SAP Build that consumes data from SAP Datasphere via an OData V4 service.

The solution was working correctly at an earlier stage, but recently we started encountering an issue during execution.

When running the skill, the request fails with the following behavior:

  • HTTP status: 403 (Access Denied)
  • The execution does not complete successfully and no data is returned

What we have verified so far:

  1. The OData service works correctly when tested independently
  2. The destination is properly configured and reachable
  3. The skill logic and parameter mapping are correct
  4. Joule correctly identifies the scenario and extracts parameters
    No significant changes were made between the working state and the current issue

Observed behavior:

  • The backend service is not reached
  • Output variables return null
  • Additional errors appear during result processing due to missing response data

Environment:

  • SAP Build (Joule Studio)
  • SAP Datasphere (OData V4)
  • Integration through Joule

At this point, the issue seems to occur specifically during the execution phase, but we have not been able to identify the root cause.

Has anyone experienced a similar issue or has suggestions on what could be causing this behavior?

Any guidance would be greatly appreciated, as this issue is currently blocking our progress.

Thank you very much in advance for your help.

entry.title: Joule Studio: Why is build_default required if standard plan exists?
entry.content:
Hi SAP Community,

I’m currently setting up Joule Studio in SAP BTP and reviewing the prerequisites.

According to the documentation, Joule Studio requires an entitlement for SAP Build Process Automation with the service plan “build_default”.

However, in my subaccount I already have the “standard” (standard-application) plan assigned, which provides full production capabilities for SAP Build Process Automation (workflows, RPA, monitoring, etc.).

From my understanding:

  • The standard plan offers full functionality for productive use

  • The build_default plan seems to provide a more limited or base-level feature set

This raises a concern, as introducing or switching to build_default could potentially limit existing capabilities.

My questions are:

  1. Is the build_default plan technically required for Joule Studio, even if the standard plan is already available?

  2. Is there a dependency or integration requirement that specifically relies on build_default?

  3. Can both plans coexist without impacting existing Build Process Automation scenarios?

  4. What is the recommended entitlement setup for using Joule Studio in a productive BTP environment?

Any clarification or best practices would be highly appreciated.

Thanks in advance!

entry.title: Integration XSUAA protected MCP-Server into custom Joule Agent
entry.content:
Hello there

I have a MCP-Server deployed to Cloud Foundry environment using FastMCP and which is protected using XSUAA from Cloud Foundry (has its own instance). Using the MCP Inspector the authentication and so on works fine.

However now I'm trying to integrate it into Joule Studio using a destination. Configuration::
- Proxy Type: Internet
- URL: <MCP-Server URL> (without /mcp)
- Authentication: OAuth2UserTokenExchange 
- Client ID, etc. from my XSUAA Instance
- Property sap-joule-studio-mcp-server: true

When I'm now trying to add my MCP-Server to my custom Agent in Joule Studio I received the error message:
Unable to find MCP server tools for destination iag-users-mcp-server. No user token (JWT) has been provided. This is strictly necessary for 'OAuth2UserTokenExchange'.

Also when I'm trying to add the property HTML5.forwardAuthToken: true I received the error message:
Internal server error. Failed to execute HTTP request during MCP server tools listing. Failed to build headers.

It seems that Joule (Studio) is not forwarding the JWT Token from IAS. Does anyone know how to integrate a MCP-Server which is protected by XSUAA?

Thanks and kind regards
Fabian

entry.title: Inquiry on SAP Build Code Licensing Models and Availability
entry.content:
Hi experts!
 
I hope you are doing well…
 
We would like to extend an inquire about the available licensing models for SAP Build Code.
 
SAP Build Code is an integrated development environment (IDE), similar to SAP Business Application Studio, that includes embedded artificial intelligence capabilities and tools (e.g., Joule for Developers). When reviewing the SAP Pricing App, we were unable to identify a SKU associated with this tool under a subscription-based licensing model. Therefore, we understand that this service may only be available under alternative licensing models (e.g., CPEA or Pay-As-You-Go). We would appreciate your confirmation on this matter, as we are currently working on an initiative with a client who plans to acquire development capabilities through a subscription model; however, when creating the opportunity or building the Bill of Materials, the Build Code service does not appear as an available option under this scheme.
 
Additionally, we would like to confirm the following: if a client wants to develop a Fiori/CAP application using Joule for Developers, is it necessary to do so through SAP Build Code? And if so, would this service only be available under the CPEA model?
 
We look forward to your response and would appreciate any documentation or resources you can share to further clarify this topic.
 
¡Kind regards! 
 
SAP Build Code SAP Business Application Studio Joule

entry.title: Joule unified setup in TDD environment (SuccessFactors and S/4H PE).
entry.content:
Dear Community,

I have a question related to Joule unified setup in TDD environment.

As we know Joule unified setup requires that all systems are managed by one IAS instance.

Now we have:

  • Cloud TDD for S/4HANA Cloud PE, 1 System (8012331)
  • Cloud TD, SF HCM Business Package (8007218)
  • Cloud TDD, Joule base entitlement (8020592)
  • Cloud TDD, Joule for developers ABAP AI (8019541)

Is it possible to move SuccessFactors from predefined IAS to IAS that is used with S/4HANA Cloud PE to use Jule TDD (unified setup) in both systems?

Or is there any other method to achieve this goal (utilize Joule TDD unified setup in TDD environment)?

entry.title: IBP Joule Setup in IAS with multiple Source/Targets
entry.content:
I am trying to complete the setup of Joule for IBP in IAS.  We already have an active setup for Joule servicing our S4 instance. 

Using the Blog as a guide - To enable the integration within IBP, when I get to the point where I am finishing "Sync Users & Roles from IBP to Build Workzone", I can complete the source system without issue - when it comes to the target system - there is already a target system for the S4 instance.  Do I need to create a create a different target system for the IBP connection or is there a way to make it work using the same target system for S4?  I attempted to add our IBP connection to the S4 target as highlighted by the red rectangle in the attachment, but it won't sync the users.

Thoughts and suggestions?

entry.title: "Destination is not a valid DGS destination" - Joule for Consultants Console custom documents setup
entry.content:
Hello Community,

 

I am trying to set up SharePoint document grounding in the Joule for Consultants Console (custom-documents section) - section user specific knowledge grounding. After connecting to my destinations-service instande all destinations show "Destination is not a valid DGS destination" regardless of configuration. I have tried OAuth2ClientCredentials and OAuth2Password, both with graph.microsoft.com as URL. The grounding-rag resource group has ext.ai.sap.com/document-grounding=true. An existing grounding pipeline with the same SharePoint destination works successfully. Even the auto-generated destinations show the same error. What exactly does the DGS validator check, and what is the correct destination configuration for this specific console?

 

Best Regards

entry.title: Destination is not a valid DGS destination - Joule for Consultants Console custom documents setup
entry.content:
Hi Community,

I am trying to set up SharePoint document grounding in the Joule for Consultants Console (specific knowledge-grounding section). All destinations show "Destination is not a valid DGS destination" regardless of configuration. I have tried OAuth2ClientCredentials and OAuth2Password, both with graph.microsoft.com as URL. The grounding-rag resource group has ext.ai.sap.com/document-grounding=true. An existing grounding pipeline with the same SharePoint destination works successfully. Even the auto-generated  destinations show the same error. What exactly does the DGS validator check, and what is the correct destination configuration for this specific console?

 

Best Regards
